1: Paul, Paulus. Of Latin origin; Paulus, the name of a Roman and of an apostle.
(a) (as adj.) enslaved, (b) (as noun) a (male) slave. From deo; a slave.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
A primary particle; but, and, etc.
From apostello; a delegate; specially, an ambassador of the Gospel; officially a commissioner of Christ.
Of Hebrew origin; Jesus, the name of our Lord and two other Israelites.
Anointed One; the Messiah, the Christ. From chrio; Anointed One, i.e. The Messiah, an epithet of Jesus.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
Faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
From eklegomai; select; by implication, favorite.
From epiginosko; recognition, i.e. full discernment, acknowledgement.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
Piety (towards God), godliness, devotion, godliness. From eusebes; piety; specially, the gospel scheme.
On, to, against, on the basis of, at.
Hope, expectation, trust, confidence. From a primary elpo; expectation or confidence.
Life, both of physical (present) and of spiritual (particularly future) existence. From zao; life.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
Not guilty of falsehood, truthful. Veracious.
From epi and the base of aggelos; to announce upon, i.e. to engage to do something, to assert something respecting oneself.
A primary preposition; fore, i.e. In front of, prior to.
A space of time or interval; by extension, an individual opportunity; by implication, delay.
Pertaining to self, i.e. One's own; by implication, private or separate.
Fitting season, season, opportunity, occasion, time. Of uncertain affinity; an occasion, i.e. Set or proper time.
To make clear (visible, manifest), make known. From phaneros; to render apparent.
A primary particle; but, and, etc.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
He, she, it, they, them, same. From the particle au; the reflexive pronoun self, used of the third person, and of the other persons.
From lego; something said; by implication, a topic, also reasoning or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, the Divine Expression.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
A proclamation, preaching. From kerusso; a proclamation.
From pistis; to have faith, i.e. Credit; by implication, to entrust.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
Instruction, command, order, authority. From epitasso; an injunction or decree; by implication, authoritativeness.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A savior, deliverer, preserver. From sozo; a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ.
4: Of Latin origin but uncertain significance; Titus, a Christian.
From the same as genesia; legitimate, i.e. Genuine.
A child, descendent, inhabitant. From the base of timoria; a child.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
Probably from sun; common, i.e. shared by all or several, or profane.
Faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness.
From chairo; graciousness, of manner or act.
Probably from a primary verb eiro; peace; by implication, prosperity.
From, away from. A primary particle; off, i.e. Away, in various senses.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
Father, (Heavenly) Father, ancestor, elder, senior. Apparently a primary word; a father.
Anointed One; the Messiah, the Christ. From chrio; Anointed One, i.e. The Messiah, an epithet of Jesus.
Of Hebrew origin; Jesus, the name of our Lord and two other Israelites.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A savior, deliverer, preserver. From sozo; a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ.
5: Accusative case of charis as preposition; through favor of, i.e. On account of.
From apo and leipo; to leave behind; by implication, to forsake.
You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
Crete. Of uncertain derivation; Crete, an island in the Mediterranean.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
From epi and a derivative of orthos; to straighten further, i.e. arrange additionally.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A primary verb; to leave, i.e. to fail or be absent.
From kata and histemi; to place down, i.e. to designate, constitute, convoy.
Comparative of presbus; older; as noun, a senior; specially, an Israelite Sanhedrist or Christian presbyter.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
A city, the inhabitants of a city. Probably from the same as polemos, or perhaps from polus; a town.
Probably adverb of comparative from hos; which how, i.e. In that manner.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
To give orders to, prescribe, arrange. From dia and tasso; to arrange thoroughly, i.e. institute, prescribe, etc.
You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.
If. A primary particle of conditionality; if, whether, that, etc.
Any one, some one, a certain one or thing. An enclitic indefinite pronoun; some or any person or object.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
A male human being; a man, husband. A primary word; a man.
One. (including the neuter Hen); a primary numeral; one.
A woman, wife, my lady. Probably from the base of ginomai; a woman; specially, a wife.
To have, hold, possess. Including an alternate form scheo skheh'-o; a primary verb; to hold.
A child, descendent, inhabitant. From the base of timoria; a child.
Trustworthy, faithful, believing. From peitho; objectively, trustworthy; subjectively, trustful.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
An accusation, charge. From kategoros; a complaint, i.e. Criminal charge.
Wantonness, profligacy, wastefulness. From a compound of a and a presumed derivative of sozo; properly, unsavedness, i.e. profligacy.
Or, than. A primary particle of distinction between two connected terms; disjunctive, or; comparative, than.
Not subject to rule, unruly. Unsubdued, i.e. Insubordinate.
For. A primary particle; properly, assigning a reason.
Probably adverb of comparative from hos; which how, i.e. In that manner.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
A household manager, a steward, guardian.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From epi and skopos; a superintendent, i.e. Christian officer in genitive case charge of a church.
Third person singular active present of deo; also deon deh-on'; neuter active participle of the same; both used impersonally; it is Necessary.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
Self-satisfied, arrogant, stubborn. From autos and the base of hedone; self-pleasing, i.e. Arrogant.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
Prone to anger, passionate. From orge; irascible.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
Given to wine, drunken, quarrelsome. From para and oinos; staying near wine, i.e. Tippling.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
A striker, contentious person, brawler. From plesso; a smiter, i.e. Pugnacious.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
Greedy, fond of base gain. From aischros and kerdos; sordid.
But, except, however. Neuter plural of allos; properly, other things, i.e. contrariwise.
Hospitable, loving strangers. From philos and xenos; fond of guests, i.e. Hospitable.
Loving what is good. From philos and agathos; fond to good, i.e. A promoter of virtue.
From the base of sozo and that of phren; safe in mind, i.e. Self-controlled.
From dike; equitable; by implication, innocent, holy.
Holy, pious, godly, beloved of God. Of uncertain affinity; properly, right, i.e. Hallowed.
Self-controlled. From en and kratos; strong in a thing, i.e. self-controlled.
From anti and the middle voice of echo; to hold oneself opposite to, i.e. adhere to; by extension to care for.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Trustworthy, faithful, believing. From peitho; objectively, trustworthy; subjectively, trustful.

A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Teaching, doctrine, what is taught. From didasko; instruction.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
(a) of persons: powerful, able, (b) of things: possible. From dunamai; powerful or capable; neuter possible.
From para and kaleo; to call near, i.e. Invite, invoke.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From hugies; to have sound health, i.e. Be well; figuratively, to be uncorrupt.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
(a) I reprove, rebuke, discipline, (b) I expose, show to be guilty. Of uncertain affinity; to confute, admonish.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
To speak or say in opposition, contradict (oppose, resist). From anti and lego; to dispute, refuse.
10: For. A primary particle; properly, assigning a reason.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Not subject to rule, unruly. Unsubdued, i.e. Insubordinate.
A vain, empty talker. From mataios and lego; an idle talker, i.e. A wrangler.
A deceiver, seducer. From phren and apate; a mind-misleader, i.e. Seducer.
Most of all, especially. Neuter plural of the superlative of an apparently primary adverb mala; most or particularly.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From out, out from among, from, suggesting from the interior outwards. A primary preposition denoting origin, from, out.

Third person singular active present of deo; also deon deh-on'; neuter active participle of the same; both used impersonally; it is Necessary.
To muzzle, silence, stop the mouth. From epi and stoma; to put something over the mouth, i.e. to silence.
Accusative case of charis as preposition; through favor of, i.e. On account of.
Base, disgraceful. From the same as aischunomai; shameful, i.e. Base.
Gain, advantage, profit. Of uncertain affinity; gain.
To overturn (lit. or met.), subvert, overthrow, corrupt. From ana and the base of trope; to overturn.
All, the whole, entire, complete. A primary word; whole or all, i.e. Complete, especially as noun or adverb.
A dwelling; by implication, a family.
To teach, direct, admonish. A prolonged form of a primary verb dao; to teach.
Third person singular active present of deo; also deon deh-on'; neuter active participle of the same; both used impersonally; it is Necessary.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.

Pertaining to self, i.e. One's own; by implication, private or separate.
From a compound of pro and phemi; a foreteller; by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet.
Answer, bid, bring word, command. A primary verb; to speak or say.
A Cretan, an inhabitant of Crete. From Krete; a Cretan, i.e. Inhabitant of Crete.
From an obsolete primary noun; ever, by qualification regularly; by implication, earnestly.
A liar, deceiver. From pseudomai; a falsifier.
Bad, evil, in the widest sense. Apparently a primary word; worthless, i.e. depraved, or injurious.
Properly: a wild beast, hence: any animal; met: a brute. Diminutive from the same as thera; a dangerous animal.
Idle, lazy, thoughtless, unprofitable, injurious. (by implication) lazy, useless.
The stomach; by analogy, the matrix; figuratively, a gourmand.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Witness, evidence, testimony, reputation. From martus; evidence given.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Unconcealed, true, true in fact, worthy of credit, truthful. TRUE.
A primary preposition denoting the channel of an act; through.
From the same as aiteo; a cause, i.e. reason, crime.
(a) I reprove, rebuke, discipline, (b) I expose, show to be guilty. Of uncertain affinity; to confute, admonish.
He, she, it, they, them, same. From the particle au; the reflexive pronoun self, used of the third person, and of the other persons.
Sharply, severely. Adverb from a derivative of a comparative of apo and temno; abruptly, i.e. Peremptorily.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.

Faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
From pros and echo; to hold the mind towards, i.e. Pay attention to, be cautious about, apply oneself to, adhere to.
Jewish, Judaic. From Ioudaios; Judaic, i.e. Resembling a Judaean.
An idle tale, fable, fanciful story. Perhaps from the same as mueo; a tale, i.e. Fiction.
An ordinance, injunction, command, law. From entellomai; injunction, i.e. An authoritative prescription.
A man, one of the human race. From aner and ops; man-faced, i.e. A human being.
To turn away, pervert, remove; I restore, replace; mid: I desert, reject. From apo and strepho; to turn away or back.

Probably from the base of ginosko; the intellect, i.e. Mind; by implication, meaning.

From a compound of the base of homou and logos; to assent, i.e. Covenant, acknowledge.

Unbelieving, disobedient, who will not be persuaded. Unpersuadable, i.e. Contumacious.
Failing to pass the test, unapproved, counterfeit. By implication, worthless.
To, towards, with. A strengthened form of pro; a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. Toward.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.

You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.

In like manner, likewise, just so. From hos and an adverb from autos; as thus, i.e. In the same way.

In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
Behavior, conduct, deportment, demeanor. From kathistemi; properly, a position or condition, i.e. demeanor.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.

And not, not even, neither�nor. From me and de; but not, not even; in a continued negation, nor.

From the same as hagios; properly, clean, i.e. innocent, modest, perfect.

From the base of peran; properly, through, i.e. Around; figuratively with respect to; used in various applications, of place, cause or time.

Pertaining to self, i.e. One's own; by implication, private or separate.

Faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
To put into order; I decorate, deck, adorn. From kosmos; to put in proper order, i.e. Decorate; specially, to snuff.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.

The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.

From epi and phaino; to shine upon, i.e. Become visible or known.
Saving, bringing salvation; subst: salvation. Neuter of the same as soteria as noun; defender or defence.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
A man, one of the human race. From aner and ops; man-faced, i.e. A human being.
From pais; to train up a child, i.e. Educate, or, discipline.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
Perhaps from a and the middle voice of rheo; to contradict, i.e. Disavow, reject, abnegate.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Impiety, irreverence, ungodliness, wickedness. From asebes; impiety, i.e. wickedness.
From kosmos; terrene, literally or figuratively.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Desire, eagerness for, inordinate desire, lust. From epithumeo; a longing.
To live, be alive. A primary verb; to live.
Soberly, with moderation, prudently. Adverb from sophron; with sound mind, i.e. Moderately.
Justly, righteously. Adverb from dikaios; equitably.
Piously, religiously. Adverb from eusebes; piously.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A primary particle of present time; now; also as noun or adjective present or immediate.
From the same as aei; properly, an age; by extension, perpetuity; by implication, the world; specially a Messianic period.
From pros and dechomai; to admit (figuratively) endurance); by implication, to await.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Happy, blessed, to be envied. A prolonged form of the poetical makar; supremely blest; by extension, fortunate, well off.
Hope, expectation, trust, confidence. From a primary elpo; expectation or confidence.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From the base of dokeo; glory, in a wide application.
Appearing, manifestation, glorious display. From epiphanes; a manifestation, i.e. the advent of Christ.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
Large, great, in the widest sense.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
A savior, deliverer, preserver. From sozo; a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ.
Of Hebrew origin; Jesus, the name of our Lord and two other Israelites.
Anointed One; the Messiah, the Christ. From chrio; Anointed One, i.e. The Messiah, an epithet of Jesus.
To offer, give; I put, place. A prolonged form of a primary verb; to give.
Gen: in behalf of; acc: above.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
To release on receipt of ransom; mid: I redeem, release by paying ransom, liberate. From lutron; to ransom.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
From, away from. A primary particle; off, i.e. Away, in various senses.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
Lawlessness, iniquity, disobedience, sin. From anomos; illegality, i.e. Violation of law or wickedness.
To cleanse, make clean, literally, ceremonially, or spiritually, according to context. From katharos; to cleanse.
Apparently a primary word; a people.
From the present participle feminine of a compound of peri and eimi; being beyond usual, i.e. Special.
One who is eagerly devoted to a person or a thing, a zealot. From zeloo; a zealot.
Properly, beautiful, but chiefly good, i.e. Valuable or virtuous.
From a primary ergo; toil; by implication, an act.
15: A prolonged form of an otherwise obsolete verb; to talk, i.e. Utter words.
From para and kaleo; to call near, i.e. Invite, invoke.
(a) I reprove, rebuke, discipline, (b) I expose, show to be guilty. Of uncertain affinity; to confute, admonish.
(a) gen: with, in company with, (b) acc: (1) behind, beyond, after, of place, (2) after, of time, with nouns, neut. of adjectives.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
Instruction, command, order, authority. From epitasso; an injunction or decree; by implication, authoritativeness.
To look down upon, despise. From peri and phroneo; to think beyond, i.e. Depreciate.
You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.

1: To remind, remember, call to mind. From hupo and mimnesko; to remind quietly, i.e. Suggest to the memory.
He, she, it, they, them, same. From the particle au; the reflexive pronoun self, used of the third person, and of the other persons.
From hupo and tasso; to subordinate; reflexively, to obey.
From archomai; a commencement, or chief.
From exesti; privilege, i.e. force, capacity, competency, freedom, or mastery, delegated influence.
From a compound of peitho and archo; to be persuaded by a ruler, i.e. to submit to authority; by analogy, to conform to advice.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Ready, prepared. From an old noun heteos; adjusted, i.e. Ready.
To, towards, with. A strengthened form of pro; a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. Toward.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
From a primary ergo; toil; by implication, an act.
From blasphemos; to vilify; specially, to speak impiously.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Peaceable, abstaining from fighting, not contentious. Peaceable.
Gentle, mild, forbearing, fair, reasonable, moderate. From epi and eiko; appropriate, i.e. mild.
To show forth, prove. From en and deiknuo; to indicate.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
Mildness, gentleness. From praus; mildness, i.e. humility.
To, towards, with. A strengthened form of pro; a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. Toward.
All, the whole, every kind of. Including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole.
A man, one of the human race. From aner and ops; man-faced, i.e. A human being.
3: For. A primary particle; properly, assigning a reason.
At one time or other, at some time, formerly. From the base of pou and te; indefinite adverb, at some time, ever.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Unbelieving, disobedient, who will not be persuaded. Unpersuadable, i.e. Contumacious.
To lead astray, deceive, cause to wander. From plane; to roam.
To be a slave, be subject to, obey, be devoted. From doulos; to be a slave to.
Various, of different colors, diverse, various. Of uncertain derivation; motley, i.e. Various in character.
Desire, eagerness for, inordinate desire, lust. From epithumeo; a longing.
From handano; sensual delight; by implication, desire.
(either trans. or intrans.), I spend time, pass time, live. From dia and ago; to pass time or life.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
From kakos; badness, i.e. depravity, or malignity, or trouble.
Envy, a grudge, spite. Probably akin to the base of phtheiro; ill-will, i.e. Jealousy.
Hateful, detestable, disgusting. From a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary stugo; hated, i.e. Odious.
To hate, detest, love less, esteem less. From a primary misos; to detest; by extension, to love less.
One another, each other. Genitive plural from allos reduplicated; one another.
A primary particle; but, and, etc.
When, at which time. From hos and te; at which too, i.e. When.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Goodness, uprightness, kindness, gentleness. From chrestos; usefulness, i.e. Morally, excellence.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.

A savior, deliverer, preserver. From sozo; a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Love of mankind, benevolence. From the same as philanthropos; fondness of mankind, i.e. Benevolence.
From epi and phaino; to shine upon, i.e. Become visible or known.
To save, heal, preserve, rescue. From a primary sos; to save, i.e. Deliver or protect.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
No, not. Also ouk, and ouch a primary word; the absolute negative adverb; no or not.
From out, out from among, from, suggesting from the interior outwards. A primary preposition denoting origin, from, out.
In, on, among. A primary preposition denoting position, and instrumentality, i.e. A relation of rest; in, at, on, by, etc.
From a primary ergo; toil; by implication, an act.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
(a) I make, manufacture, construct, (b) I do, act, cause. Apparently a prolonged form of an obsolete primary; to make or do.
But, except, however. Neuter plural of allos; properly, other things, i.e. contrariwise.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
He, she, it, they, them, same. From the particle au; the reflexive pronoun self, used of the third person, and of the other persons.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Pity, mercy, compassion. Of uncertain affinity; compassion.
A primary preposition denoting the channel of an act; through.
A bath (of water, not the vessel), water for washing, washing. From louo; a bath, i.e., baptism.
A new birth, regeneration, renewal. From palin and genesis; rebirth, i.e. spiritual renovation; specially, Messianic restoration.
Renewing; a renewal or change of heart and life. From anakainoo; renovation.
Set apart by (or for) God, holy, sacred. From hagos; sacred.
Or ekchuno ek-khoo'-no from ek and cheo; to pour forth; figuratively, to bestow.
On, to, against, on the basis of, at.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
Richly, abundantly. Adverb from plousios; copiously.
A primary preposition denoting the channel of an act; through.
Of Hebrew origin; Jesus, the name of our Lord and two other Israelites.
Anointed One; the Messiah, the Christ. From chrio; Anointed One, i.e. The Messiah, an epithet of Jesus.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A savior, deliverer, preserver. From sozo; a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
From dikaios; to render just or innocent.
That, that one there, yonder. From ekei; that one (neuter) thing); often intensified by the article prefixed.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From chairo; graciousness, of manner or act.
A prolongation and middle voice form of a primary verb; to cause to be, i.e. to become, used with great latitude.
From kleros and the base of nomos (reflexively) getting by apportionment); a sharer by lot, i.e. Inheritor; by implication, a possessor.
A primary particle; down, in varied relations (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined).
Hope, expectation, trust, confidence. From a primary elpo; expectation or confidence.
Life, both of physical (present) and of spiritual (particularly future) existence. From zao; life.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From lego; something said; by implication, a topic, also reasoning or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, the Divine Expression.
Trustworthy, faithful, believing. From peitho; objectively, trustworthy; subjectively, trustful.
To will, intend, desire, wish. Middle voice of a primary verb; to will, i.e. be willing.
You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.
To assert emphatically. Middle voice of a compound of dia and bebaioo; to confirm thoroughly, i.e. Asseverate.
From the base of peran; properly, through, i.e. Around; figuratively with respect to; used in various applications, of place, cause or time.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From pistis; to have faith, i.e. Credit; by implication, to entrust.
A deity, especially the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very.
To be thoughtful, careful, give heed. From a derivative of phren; to exercise thought, i.e. Be anxious.
From pro and histemi; to stand before, i.e. to preside, or to practise.
Properly, beautiful, but chiefly good, i.e. Valuable or virtuous.
From a primary ergo; toil; by implication, an act.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Properly, beautiful, but chiefly good, i.e. Valuable or virtuous.
Profitable, beneficial, useful. From a form of ophelos; helpful or serviceable, i.e. Advantageous.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
A man, one of the human race. From aner and ops; man-faced, i.e. A human being.
9: A primary particle; but, and, etc.
From peri and histemi; to stand all around, i.e. to be a bystander, or to keep away from.
Probably from the base of musterion; dull or stupid, i.e. Heedless, blockhead, absurd.
A question, debate, controversy; a seeking, search. From zeteo; a searching, i.e. A dispute or its theme.
Genealogy. From the same as genealogeo; tracing by generations, i.e. genealogy.
Contention, strife, wrangling. Of uncertain affinity; a quarrel, i.e. wrangling.
From machomai; a battle, i.e. controversy.
From nomos; according to law, i.e. Legal; as noun, an expert in the law.
For. A primary particle; properly, assigning a reason.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
Vain, unreal, ineffectual, unproductive; practically: godless. From the base of maten; empty, i.e. profitless, or, an idol.
From para and the middle voice of aiteo; to beg off, i.e. Deprecate, decline, shun.
Disposed to form sects, sectarian, heretical, factious. From the same as hairetizo; a schismatic.
A man, one of the human race. From aner and ops; man-faced, i.e. A human being.
(a) gen: with, in company with, (b) acc: (1) behind, beyond, after, of place, (2) after, of time, with nouns, neut. of adjectives.
One. (including the neuter Hen); a primary numeral; one.
Second; with the article: in the second place, for the second time. As the compare of duo; second.
A warning, admonition, counsel. From nous and a derivative of tithemi; calling attention to, i.e. mild rebuke or warning.
Neuter of hostis as conjunction; demonstrative, that; causative, because.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
(including the other inflections); from toi and houtos; truly this, i.e. Of this sort (to denote character or individuality).
To change for the worse, corrupt, pervert. From ek and strepho; to pervert.
Perhaps from a and the base of meros; properly, to miss the mark, i.e. to err, especially to sin.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.
(perhaps a new coinage), self-condemned. From autos and a derivative or katakrino; self-condemned.
12: When, whenever. From hote and an; whenever; also causatively inasmuch as.
To send, transmit, permit to go, put forth.
Artemas, a Christian in Rome. Contracted from a compound of Artemis and doron; gift of Artemis; Artemas, a Christian.
Or, than. A primary particle of distinction between two connected terms; disjunctive, or; comparative, than.
From a derivative of tugchano; fortuitous, i.e. Fortunate; Tychicus, a Christian.
To, towards, with. A strengthened form of pro; a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. Toward.
You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.
To hasten, be eager, be zealous. From spoude; to use speed, i.e. To make effort, be prompt or earnest.
To, towards, with. A strengthened form of pro; a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. Toward.
I, the first-person pronoun. A primary pronoun of the first person I.
A primary preposition; to or into, of place, time, or purpose; also in adverbial phrases.
Nicopolis, a city of Macedonia. From nikos and polis; victorious city; Nicopolis, a place in Macedonia.
For. A primary particle; properly, assigning a reason.
Properly, to distinguish, i.e. Decide; by implication, to try, condemn, punish.
To pass the winter. From para and cheimazo; to winter near, i.e. Stay with over the rainy season.
(a) there, yonder, in that place, (b) thither, there. Of uncertain affinity; there; by extension, thither.
Diligently, earnestly, zealously. Adverb from spoudaios; earnestly, promptly.
To send forward, accompany, equip for a journey. From pro and pempo; to send forward, i.e. Escort or aid in travel.
Zenas, a lawyer in Rome. Probably contracted from a poetic form of Zeus and doron; Jove-given; Zenas, a Christian.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From nomos; according to law, i.e. Legal; as noun, an expert in the law.
Apollos, a Jew of Alexandria. Probably from the same as Apollonia; Apollos, an Israelite.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
He, she, it, they, them, same. From the particle au; the reflexive pronoun self, used of the third person, and of the other persons.
A primary verb; to leave, i.e. to fail or be absent.
A primary particle; but, and, etc.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
Our, our own. From thnetos; our.
Prolongation from a primary verb, another form of which, matheo, is used as an alternate in certain tenses; to learn.
From pro and histemi; to stand before, i.e. to preside, or to practise.
Properly, beautiful, but chiefly good, i.e. Valuable or virtuous.
From a primary ergo; toil; by implication, an act.
A primary preposition; to or into, of place, time, or purpose; also in adverbial phrases.
Necessary, essential, intimate, right, proper. From anagke; necessary; by implication, close.
The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.
From the base of chraomai or chre; employment, i.e. An affair; also occasion, demand, requirement or destitution.
In order that, so that. Probably from the same as the former part of heautou; in order that.
Not, lest. A primary particle of qualified negation; not, lest; also (whereas ou expects an affirmative one) whether.
I am, exist. The first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist.

The, the definite article. Including the feminine he, and the neuter to in all their inflections; the definite article; the.

You. The person pronoun of the second person singular; thou.
